falcon_serial_log_priority.description: Set the priority for writing the Falcon serial log

falcon_support_xa.name: falcon_support_xa
falcon_support_xa.description: Enables two-phase commit for Falcon tables

falcon_use_deferred_index_hash.name: falcon_use_deferred_index_hash
falcon_use_deferred_index_hash.description: Use a deferred index hash lookup

falcon_use_sectorcache.name: falcon_use_sectorcache
falcon_use_sectorcache.description: Use the sector cache for reading blocks from disk

flush.name: flush
flush.description: Flush tables to disk between SQL statements

flush_time.name: flush_time
flush_time.description: A dedicated thread is created to flush all tables at the given interval

foreign_key_checks.name: foreign_key_checks
foreign_key_checks.description: If set to 1 (the default), foreign key constraints for InnoDB tables are checked

ft_boolean_syntax.name: ft_boolean_syntax
ft_boolean_syntax.description: List of operators for MATCH ... AGAINST ( ... IN BOOLEAN MODE)

ft_max_word_len.name: ft_max_word_len
ft_max_word_len.description: The maximum length of the word to be included in a FULLTEXT index. Note: FULLTEXT indexes must be rebuilt after changing this variable

ft_min_word_len.name: ft_min_word_len
ft_min_word_len.description: The minimum length of the word to be included in a FULLTEXT index. Note: FULLTEXT indexes must be rebuilt after changing this variable

ft_query_expansion_limit.name: ft_query_expansion_limit
ft_query_expansion_limit.description: Number of best matches to use for query expansion

ft_stopword_file.name: ft_stopword_file
ft_stopword_file.description: Use stopwords from this file instead of built-in list

general_log_file.name: general_log_file
general_log_file.description: The name of the general query log file. The path is relative to the data directory unless an absolute path is given.

group_concat_max_len.name: group_concat_max_len
group_concat_max_len.description: The maximum length of the result of function group_concat

gtid-mode.name: gtid-mode
gtid-mode.description: Controls whether GTID based logging is enabled and what type of transactions the logs can contain

gtid_done.name: gtid_done
gtid_done.description: Obsolete: Replaced by gtid_executed in MySQL 5.6.9

gtid_executed.name: gtid_executed
gtid_executed.description: Global: All GTIDs in the binary log (global) or current transaction (session). Read-only.

gtid_executed_compression_period.name: gtid_executed_compression_period
gtid_executed_compression_period.description: Compress gtid_executed table each time this many transactions have occurred. 0 means never compress this table. Applies only when binary logging is disabled.

gtid_lost.name: gtid_lost
gtid_lost.description: Obsolete: Replaced by gtid_purged in MySQL 5.6.9

gtid_mode.name: gtid_mode
gtid_mode.description: Whether GTID-based replication is enabled. Values: OFF, OFF_PERMISSIVE, ON_PERMISSIVE, ON.

gtid_next.name: gtid_next
gtid_next.description: Specifies the GTID for the next statement to execute; see documentation for details

gtid_owned.name: gtid_owned
gtid_owned.description: The set of GTIDs owned by this client (session), or by all clients, together with the thread ID of the owner (global). Read-only.

gtid_purged.name: gtid_purged
gtid_purged.description: The set of all GTIDs that have been purged from the binary log

have_community_features.name: have_community_features
have_community_features.description: Whether statement profiling capability is available

have_compress.name: have_compress
have_compress.description: Availability of the zlib compression library

have_crypt.name: have_crypt
have_crypt.description: Availability of the crypt() system call

have_dynamic_loading.name: have_dynamic_loading
have_dynamic_loading.description: Whether mysqld supports dynamic loading of plugins

have_geometry.name: have_geometry
have_geometry.description: Whether mysqld supports spatial data types

have_ndbcluster.name: have_ndbcluster
have_ndbcluster.description: Whether mysqld supports NDB Cluster tables (set by --ndbcluster option)

have_profiling.name: have_profiling
have_profiling.description: Whether statement profiling capability is available

have_raid.name: have_raid
have_raid.description: Whether mysqld supports the raid option

have_row_based_replication.name: have_row_based_replication
have_row_based_replication.description: Shows whether row-based replication is supported

have_rtree_keys.name: have_rtree_keys
have_rtree_keys.description: YES if RTREE indexes are available, NO if not. (These are used for spatial indexes in MyISAM tables.)

have_statement_timeout.name: have_statement_timeout
have_statement_timeout.description: Whether statement execution timeout is available

histogram_generation_max_mem_size.name: histogram_generation_max_mem_size
histogram_generation_max_mem_size.description: Maximum memory for creating histogram statistics

hostname.name: hostname
hostname.description: The name of the server host

identity.name: identity
identity.description: This variable is a synonym for the LAST_INSERT_ID variable. It exists for compatibility with other database systems

information_schema_stats_expiry.name: information_schema_stats_expiry
information_schema_stats_expiry.description: Expiration setting for cached table statistics

init-file.name: init-file
init-file.description: Read SQL statements from this file at startup

init_connect.name: init_connect
init_connect.description: Statements that are executed for each new connection

init_slave.name: init_slave
init_slave.description: Statements that are executed when a slave connects to a master

innodb_adaptive_flushing.name: innodb_adaptive_flushing
innodb_adaptive_flushing.description: Control InnoDB adaptive flushing of dirty pages

innodb_adaptive_flushing_lwm.name: innodb_adaptive_flushing_lwm
innodb_adaptive_flushing_lwm.description: Low water mark representing percentage of redo log capacity at which adaptive flushing is enabled

innodb_adaptive_hash_index.name: innodb_adaptive_hash_index
innodb_adaptive_hash_index.description: Enable or disable InnoDB adaptive hash indexes

innodb_adaptive_hash_index_parts.name: innodb_adaptive_hash_index_parts
innodb_adaptive_hash_index_parts.description: Partitions the adaptive hash index search system into n partitions, with each partition protected by a separate latch. Each index is bound to a specific partition based on space ID and index ID attributes.

innodb_adaptive_max_sleep_delay.name: innodb_adaptive_max_sleep_delay
innodb_adaptive_max_sleep_delay.description: Allows InnoDB to automatically adjust the value of innodb_thread_sleep_delay up or down according to the current workload

innodb_api_bk_commit_interval.name: innodb_api_bk_commit_interval
innodb_api_bk_commit_interval.description: How often to auto-commit idle connections that use the InnoDB memcached interface, in seconds

innodb_api_enable_binlog.name: innodb_api_enable_binlog
innodb_api_enable_binlog.description: Lets you use the InnoDB memcached plugin with the MySQL binary log

innodb_api_enable_mdl.name: innodb_api_enable_mdl
innodb_api_enable_mdl.description: Locks the table used by the InnoDB memcached plugin, so that it cannot be dropped or altered by DDL through the SQL interface

innodb_api_trx_level.name: innodb_api_trx_level
innodb_api_trx_level.description: Lets you control the transaction isolation level on queries processed by the memcached interface

innodb_autoextend_increment.name: innodb_autoextend_increment
innodb_autoextend_increment.description: Data file autoextend increment in megabytes

innodb_background_drop_list_empty.name: innodb_background_drop_list_empty
innodb_background_drop_list_empty.description: This debug option delays table creation until the background drop list is empty

innodb_buffer_pool_awe_mem_mb.name: innodb_buffer_pool_awe_mem_mb
innodb_buffer_pool_awe_mem_mb.description: If Windows AWE is used, size in Megabytes of InnoDB buffer pool allocated from the AWE memory

innodb_buffer_pool_chunk_size.name: innodb_buffer_pool_chunk_size
innodb_buffer_pool_chunk_size.description: Defines the chunk size that is used when resizing the buffer pool

innodb_buffer_pool_debug.name: innodb_buffer_pool_debug
innodb_buffer_pool_debug.description: Permits multiple buffer pool instances when the buffer pool is less than 1GB in size

innodb_buffer_pool_dump_at_shutdown.name: innodb_buffer_pool_dump_at_shutdown
innodb_buffer_pool_dump_at_shutdown.description: Specifies whether to record the pages cached in the InnoDB buffer pool when the MySQL server is shut down, to shorten the warmup process at the next restart

innodb_buffer_pool_dump_now.name: innodb_buffer_pool_dump_now
innodb_buffer_pool_dump_now.description: Immediately records the pages cached in the InnoDB buffer pool

innodb_buffer_pool_dump_pct.name: innodb_buffer_pool_dump_pct
innodb_buffer_pool_dump_pct.description: Specifies the percentage of the most recently used pages for each buffer pool to read out and dump

innodb_buffer_pool_filename.name: innodb_buffer_pool_filename
innodb_buffer_pool_filename.description: Specifies the file that holds the list of page numbers produced by innodb_buffer_pool_dump_at_shutdown or innodb_buffer_pool_dump_now

innodb_buffer_pool_instances.name: innodb_buffer_pool_instances
innodb_buffer_pool_instances.description: The number of buffer pool instances for InnoDB. Each instance manages its own free lists and flush lists.

innodb_buffer_pool_load_abort.name: innodb_buffer_pool_load_abort
innodb_buffer_pool_load_abort.description: Interrupts process of restoring InnoDB buffer pool contents triggered by innodb_buffer_pool_load_at_startup or innodb_buffer_pool_load_now

innodb_buffer_pool_load_at_startup.name: innodb_buffer_pool_load_at_startup
innodb_buffer_pool_load_at_startup.description: Specifies that, on MySQL server startup, the InnoDB buffer pool is automatically "warmed up" by loading the same pages it held at an earlier time

innodb_buffer_pool_load_now.name: innodb_buffer_pool_load_now
innodb_buffer_pool_load_now.description: Immediately "warms up" the InnoDB buffer pool by loading a set of data pages, without waiting for a server restart

innodb_buffer_pool_size.name: innodb_buffer_pool_size
innodb_buffer_pool_size.description: The size of the InnoDB buffer pool, the memory area where InnoDB caches table and index data. Essential for read performance.

innodb_change_buffer_max_size.name: innodb_change_buffer_max_size
innodb_change_buffer_max_size.description: Maximum size for the InnoDB change buffer, as a percentage of the total size of buffer pool

innodb_change_buffering_debug.name: innodb_change_buffering_debug
innodb_change_buffering_debug.description: Sets a debug flag for InnoDB change buffering

innodb_checkpoint_disabled.name: innodb_checkpoint_disabled
innodb_checkpoint_disabled.description: Disables checkpoints so that a deliberate server exit always initiates recovery

innodb_checksum_algorithm.name: innodb_checksum_algorithm
innodb_checksum_algorithm.description: Specifies how to generate and verify the checksum stored in each disk block of each InnoDB tablespace

innodb_cmp_per_index_enabled.name: innodb_cmp_per_index_enabled
innodb_cmp_per_index_enabled.description: Enables per-index compression-related statistics in the INFORMATION_SCHEMA.INNODB_CMP_PER_INDEX table

innodb_commit_concurrency.name: innodb_commit_concurrency
innodb_commit_concurrency.description: Helps in performance tuning in heavily concurrent environments

innodb_compress_debug.name: innodb_compress_debug
innodb_compress_debug.description: Compresses all tables using a specified compression algorithm

innodb_compression_failure_threshold_pct.name: innodb_compression_failure_threshold_pct
innodb_compression_failure_threshold_pct.description: Sets the cutoff point at which MySQL begins adding padding within compressed pages to avoid expensive compression failures

innodb_compression_level.name: innodb_compression_level
innodb_compression_level.description: Specifies the level of zlib compression to use for InnoDB compressed tables and indexes

innodb_compression_pad_pct_max.name: innodb_compression_pad_pct_max
innodb_compression_pad_pct_max.description: Specifies the maximum percentage that can be reserved as free space within each compressed page, to avoid compression failures when tightly packed data is recompressed

innodb_concurrency_tickets.name: innodb_concurrency_tickets
innodb_concurrency_tickets.description: Number of times a thread is allowed to enter InnoDB within the same SQL query after it has once got the ticket

innodb_data_file_path.name: innodb_data_file_path
innodb_data_file_path.description: The paths and sizes of the InnoDB system tablespace data files. Set at server startup only.

innodb_data_home_dir.name: innodb_data_home_dir
innodb_data_home_dir.description: The common part for InnoDB table spaces

innodb_ddl_log_crash_reset_debug.name: innodb_ddl_log_crash_reset_debug
innodb_ddl_log_crash_reset_debug.description: A debug option that resets DDL log crash injection counters

innodb_dedicated_server.name: innodb_dedicated_server
innodb_dedicated_server.description: Enables automatic configuration of buffer pool size, log file size, and flush method

innodb_default_row_format.name: innodb_default_row_format
innodb_default_row_format.description: Defines the default row format (ROW_FORMAT) for InnoDB tables

innodb_directories.name: innodb_directories
innodb_directories.description: Defines directories to scan at startup for tablespace data files

innodb_disable_sort_file_cache.name: innodb_disable_sort_file_cache
innodb_disable_sort_file_cache.description: Disable OS file system cache for merge-sort temporary files

innodb_disable_resize_buffer_pool_debug.name: innodb_disable_resize_buffer_pool_debug
innodb_disable_resize_buffer_pool_debug.description: Disables resizing of the InnoDB buffer pool

innodb_extra_dirty_writes.name: innodb_extra_dirty_writes
innodb_extra_dirty_writes.description: Whether to flush dirty buffer pages when the percentage of dirty pages is less than the maximum dirty percent

innodb_fast_shutdown.name: innodb_fast_shutdown
innodb_fast_shutdown.description: Speeds up the shutdown process of the InnoDB storage engine

innodb_fil_make_page_dirty_debug.name: innodb_fil_make_page_dirty_debug
innodb_fil_make_page_dirty_debug.description: Dirties the first page of the specified tablespace

innodb_file_format.name: innodb_file_format
innodb_file_format.description: The format for new InnoDB tables

innodb_file_format_check.name: innodb_file_format_check
innodb_file_format_check.description: Whether InnoDB performs file format compatibility checking

innodb_file_format_max.name: innodb_file_format_max
innodb_file_format_max.description: The file format tag in the shared tablespace

innodb_file_io_threads.name: innodb_file_io_threads
innodb_file_io_threads.description: Number of file I/O threads in InnoDB

innodb_file_per_table.name: innodb_file_per_table
innodb_file_per_table.description: When enabled, InnoDB stores each table's data and indexes in its own file in the database directory.

innodb_fill_factor.name: innodb_fill_factor
innodb_fill_factor.description: Defines the percentage B-tree leaf and non-leaf page space that is to be filled with data. The remaining space is reserved for future growth.

innodb_flush_log_at_timeout.name: innodb_flush_log_at_timeout
innodb_flush_log_at_timeout.description: Write and flush logs every N seconds

innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit.name: innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit
innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit.description: Set to 0 (write and flush once per second), 1 (write and flush at each commit) or 2 (write at commit, flush once per second)

innodb_flush_neighbors.name: innodb_flush_neighbors
innodb_flush_neighbors.description: Specifies whether or not flushing a page from the InnoDB buffer pool also flushes other dirty pages in the same extent

innodb_flush_sync.name: innodb_flush_sync
innodb_flush_sync.description: Enable innodb_flush_sync to ignore the innodb_io_capacity setting for bursts of I/O activity that occur at checkpoints. Disable innodb_flush_sync to adhere to the limit on I/O activity defined by the innodb_io_capacity setting.

innodb_flushing_avg_loops.name: innodb_flushing_avg_loops
innodb_flushing_avg_loops.description: Number of iterations for which InnoDB keeps the previously calculated snapshot of the flushing state, controlling how quickly adaptive flushing responds to changing workloads

innodb_force_load_corrupted.name: innodb_force_load_corrupted
innodb_force_load_corrupted.description: Lets InnoDB load tables at startup that are marked as corrupted; use only during troubleshooting

innodb_force_recovery.name: innodb_force_recovery
innodb_force_recovery.description: Helps to save your data in case the disk image of the database becomes corrupt

innodb_ft_aux_table.name: innodb_ft_aux_table
innodb_ft_aux_table.description: Specifies which InnoDB table with a FULLTEXT index to examine by querying several innodb_ft_* tables in the information_schema

innodb_ft_cache_size.name: innodb_ft_cache_size
innodb_ft_cache_size.description: Size of the cache that holds a parsed document in memory while creating an InnoDB FULLTEXT index

innodb_ft_enable_diag_print.name: innodb_ft_enable_diag_print
innodb_ft_enable_diag_print.description: Whether to enable additional full-text search diagnostic output

innodb_ft_enable_stopword.name: innodb_ft_enable_stopword
innodb_ft_enable_stopword.description: During creation of an InnoDB FULLTEXT index, omits stopwords from the search index

innodb_ft_max_token_size.name: innodb_ft_max_token_size
innodb_ft_max_token_size.description: Maximum length of words that are stored in an InnoDB FULLTEXT index

innodb_ft_min_token_size.name: innodb_ft_min_token_size
innodb_ft_min_token_size.description: Minimum length of words that are stored in an InnoDB FULLTEXT index

innodb_ft_num_word_optimize.name: innodb_ft_num_word_optimize
innodb_ft_num_word_optimize.description: Number of words to process during each OPTIMIZE TABLE operation on an InnoDB FULLTEXT index

innodb_ft_result_cache_limit.name: innodb_ft_result_cache_limit
innodb_ft_result_cache_limit.description: The InnoDB FULLTEXT search query result cache limit

innodb_ft_server_stopword_table.name: innodb_ft_server_stopword_table
innodb_ft_server_stopword_table.description: Specifies a table holding a list of stopwords for InnoDB FULLTEXT indexes, which overrides the default stopword list and can be overridden by innodb_ft_user_stopword_table

innodb_ft_sort_pll_degree.name: innodb_ft_sort_pll_degree
innodb_ft_sort_pll_degree.description: Number of threads used to create an InnoDB FULLTEXT index in parallel, when building a search index for a large table

innodb_ft_total_cache_size.name: innodb_ft_total_cache_size
innodb_ft_total_cache_size.description: The total memory allocated for the InnoDB FULLTEXT search index cache

innodb_ft_user_stopword_table.name: innodb_ft_user_stopword_table
innodb_ft_user_stopword_table.description: Specifies a table holding a list of stopwords for InnoDB FULLTEXT indexes, which overrides the default stopword list and also innodb_ft_server_stopword_table

innodb_io_capacity.name: innodb_io_capacity
innodb_io_capacity.description: The maximum number of I/O operations per second that InnoDB should perform for background tasks.

innodb_io_capacity_max.name: innodb_io_capacity_max
innodb_io_capacity_max.description: The maximum number of I/O operations per second that InnoDB can handle during emergency background tasks.

innodb_large_prefix.name: innodb_large_prefix
innodb_large_prefix.description: Enables longer keys for column prefix indexes

innodb_limit_optimistic_insert_debug.name: innodb_limit_optimistic_insert_debug
innodb_limit_optimistic_insert_debug.description: Limits the number of records per B-tree page

innodb_lock_wait_timeout.name: innodb_lock_wait_timeout
innodb_lock_wait_timeout.description: Timeout in seconds an InnoDB transaction may wait for a lock before a rollback occurs

innodb_locks_unsafe_for_binlog.name: innodb_locks_unsafe_for_binlog
innodb_locks_unsafe_for_binlog.description: Force InnoDB not to use next-key locking. Instead use only row-level locking

innodb_log_arch_dir.name: innodb_log_arch_dir
innodb_log_arch_dir.description: Where full logs should be archived

innodb_log_buffer_size.name: innodb_log_buffer_size
innodb_log_buffer_size.description: The size of the buffer that InnoDB uses to write to the log files on disk. Larger values reduce disk I/O during transactions.

innodb_log_checksum_algorithm.name: innodb_log_checksum_algorithm
innodb_log_checksum_algorithm.description: Specifies how to generate and verify the checksum stored in each redo log disk block

innodb_log_checksums.name: innodb_log_checksums
innodb_log_checksums.description: Enables or disables checksums for redo log pages

innodb_log_compressed_pages.name: innodb_log_compressed_pages
innodb_log_compressed_pages.description: Specifies whether images of re-compressed pages are stored in InnoDB redo logs

innodb_log_file_size.name: innodb_log_file_size
innodb_log_file_size.description: The size in bytes of each InnoDB redo log file. The combined size of all redo log files is innodb_log_file_size * innodb_log_files_in_group.

innodb_log_files_in_group.name: innodb_log_files_in_group
innodb_log_files_in_group.description: Number of InnoDB log files in the log group

innodb_log_spin_cpu_abs_lwm.name: innodb_log_spin_cpu_abs_lwm
innodb_log_spin_cpu_abs_lwm.description: Minimum amount of CPU usage below which user threads no longer spin while waiting for flushed redo

innodb_log_spin_cpu_pct_hwm.name: innodb_log_spin_cpu_pct_hwm
innodb_log_spin_cpu_pct_hwm.description: Maximum amount of CPU usage above which user threads no longer spin while waiting for flushed redo

innodb_log_wait_for_flush_spin_hwm.name: innodb_log_wait_for_flush_spin_hwm
innodb_log_wait_for_flush_spin_hwm.description: The maximum average log flush time beyond which user threads no longer spin while waiting for flushed redo

innodb_log_write_ahead_size.name: innodb_log_write_ahead_size
innodb_log_write_ahead_size.description: The write-ahead block size for the redo log

innodb_lru_scan_depth.name: innodb_lru_scan_depth
innodb_lru_scan_depth.description: Influences the algorithms and heuristics for the flush operation for the InnoDB buffer pool

innodb_max_dirty_pages_pct.name: innodb_max_dirty_pages_pct
innodb_max_dirty_pages_pct.description: Percentage of dirty pages allowed in buffer pool

innodb_max_dirty_pages_pct_lwm.name: innodb_max_dirty_pages_pct_lwm
innodb_max_dirty_pages_pct_lwm.description: Low water mark representing percentage of dirty pages where preflushing is enabled to control the dirty page ratio

innodb_max_merged_io.name: innodb_max_merged_io
innodb_max_merged_io.description: The maximum number of background I/O requests to merge to issue a larger I/O request

innodb_max_purge_lag.name: innodb_max_purge_lag
innodb_max_purge_lag.description: Desired maximum length of the purge queue (0 = no limit)

innodb_max_purge_lag_delay.name: innodb_max_purge_lag_delay
innodb_max_purge_lag_delay.description: Specifies the maximum delay in milliseconds for the formula calculated using the innodb_max_purge_lag configuration option

innodb_max_undo_log_size.name: innodb_max_undo_log_size
innodb_max_undo_log_size.description: Sets the threshold for truncating the InnoDB undo log

innodb_additional_mem_pool_size.name: innodb_additional_mem_pool_size
innodb_additional_mem_pool_size.description: Size of a memory pool InnoDB uses to store data dictionary information and other internal data structures

innodb_merge_threshold_set_all_debug.name: innodb_merge_threshold_set_all_debug
innodb_merge_threshold_set_all_debug.description: Overrides the current MERGE_THRESHOLD setting with the specified value for all indexes that are currently in the dictionary cache

innodb_monitor_disable.name: innodb_monitor_disable
innodb_monitor_disable.description: Turns off one or more counters in the information_schema.innodb_metrics table

innodb_monitor_enable.name: innodb_monitor_enable
innodb_monitor_enable.description: Turns on one or more counters in the information_schema.innodb_metrics table

innodb_monitor_reset.name: innodb_monitor_reset
innodb_monitor_reset.description: Resets to zero the count value for one or more counters in the information_schema.innodb_metrics table

innodb_monitor_reset_all.name: innodb_monitor_reset_all
innodb_monitor_reset_all.description: Resets all values (minimum, maximum, and so on) for one or more counters in the information_schema.innodb_metrics table

innodb_numa_interleave.name: innodb_numa_interleave
innodb_numa_interleave.description: Enables the NUMA MPOL_INTERLEAVE memory policy for allocation of the InnoDB buffer pool

innodb_old_blocks_pct.name: innodb_old_blocks_pct
innodb_old_blocks_pct.description: Percentage of the InnoDB buffer pool to reserve for old blocks

innodb_old_blocks_time.name: innodb_old_blocks_time
innodb_old_blocks_time.description: How long (in ms) blocks must remain in old end of InnoDB buffer pool before moving to new end

innodb_online_alter_log_max_size.name: innodb_online_alter_log_max_size
innodb_online_alter_log_max_size.description: Specifies an upper limit on size of the temporary log files used during online DDL operations for InnoDB tables

innodb_open_files.name: innodb_open_files
innodb_open_files.description: The maximum number of files that InnoDB keeps open at the same time

innodb_optimize_fulltext_only.name: innodb_optimize_fulltext_only
innodb_optimize_fulltext_only.description: Makes the OPTIMIZE TABLE statement for an InnoDB table process the newly added, deleted, and updated token data for a FULLTEXT index, rather than reorganizing the data in the clustered index of the table

innodb_optimize_point_storage.name: innodb_optimize_point_storage
innodb_optimize_point_storage.description: Enable this option to store POINT data as fixed-length data rather than a variable-length data

innodb_page_size.name: innodb_page_size
innodb_page_size.description: Specifies the page size for all InnoDB tablespaces in an instance

innodb_print_all_deadlocks.name: innodb_print_all_deadlocks
innodb_print_all_deadlocks.description: During shutdown, prints information about all InnoDB deadlocks to the server error log

innodb_print_ddl_logs.name: innodb_print_ddl_logs
innodb_print_ddl_logs.description: Whether or not to print DDL logs to the error log

innodb_purge_batch_size.name: innodb_purge_batch_size
innodb_purge_batch_size.description: Specifies the number of InnoDB redo logs that trigger a purge operation

innodb_purge_rseg_truncate_frequency.name: innodb_purge_rseg_truncate_frequency
innodb_purge_rseg_truncate_frequency.description: The rate at which undo log purge should be invoked as part of the purge action. A value of n invokes undo log purge on every nth iteration of purge invocation.

innodb_purge_threads.name: innodb_purge_threads
innodb_purge_threads.description: Specifies whether the InnoDB purge operation should be performed in one or more separate threads. By default, this operation is part of the InnoDB master thread.

innodb_random_read_ahead.name: innodb_random_read_ahead
innodb_random_read_ahead.description: Enables the random read-ahead technique for optimizing InnoDB I/O

innodb_read_ahead_threshold.name: innodb_read_ahead_threshold
innodb_read_ahead_threshold.description: The sensitivity of InnoDB linear read-ahead

innodb_read_io_threads.name: innodb_read_io_threads
innodb_read_io_threads.description: The number of I/O threads for read operations in InnoDB. Range is 1-64.

innodb_read_only.name: innodb_read_only

innodb_redo_log_encrypt.name: innodb_redo_log_encrypt
innodb_redo_log_encrypt.description: Controls encryption of redo log data for encrypted tablepaces

innodb_replication_delay.name: innodb_replication_delay
innodb_replication_delay.description: The slave server replication thread delay

innodb_rollback_on_timeout.name: innodb_rollback_on_timeout
innodb_rollback_on_timeout.description: Roll back entire transaction on transaction timeout, not just last statement

innodb_rollback_segments.name: innodb_rollback_segments
innodb_rollback_segments.description: Defines how many of the rollback segments in the system tablespace that InnoDB uses within a transaction

innodb_scan_directories.name: innodb_scan_directories
innodb_scan_directories.description: Defines directories to scan for tablespace files during InnoDB recovery

innodb_sort_buffer_size.name: innodb_sort_buffer_size
innodb_sort_buffer_size.description: Specifies size of a buffer used for sorting data during creation of an InnoDB index

innodb_spin_wait_delay.name: innodb_spin_wait_delay
innodb_spin_wait_delay.description: The maximum delay between polls for a spin lock

innodb_stats_auto_recalc.name: innodb_stats_auto_recalc
innodb_stats_auto_recalc.description: Causes InnoDB to automatically recalculate persistent statistics after the data in a table is changed substantially

innodb_stats_include_delete_marked.name: innodb_stats_include_delete_marked
innodb_stats_include_delete_marked.description: Include delete-marked records when calculating persistent InnoDB statistics

innodb_stats_method.name: innodb_stats_method
innodb_stats_method.description: Specifies how InnoDB index statistics collection code should treat NULLs

innodb_stats_on_metadata.name: innodb_stats_on_metadata
innodb_stats_on_metadata.description: When enabled, InnoDB updates statistics when metadata statements are executed. When disabled, statistics are updated only when needed.

innodb_stats_persistent.name: innodb_stats_persistent
innodb_stats_persistent.description: Turns on the InnoDB persistent statistics feature

innodb_stats_persistent_sample_pages.name: innodb_stats_persistent_sample_pages
innodb_stats_persistent_sample_pages.description: Number of pages to sample in each InnoDB index, when the persistent statistics feature is also enabled

innodb_stats_sample_pages.description: Number of index pages to sample for index distribution statistics

innodb_stats_transient_sample_pages.name: innodb_stats_transient_sample_pages
innodb_stats_transient_sample_pages.description: Number of pages to sample in each InnoDB index, when the persistent statistics feature is turned off (the default setting)

innodb_status_output.name: innodb_status_output
innodb_status_output.description: Used to enable or disable periodic output for the standard InnoDB Monitor. Also used in combination with innodb_status_output_locks to enable and disable periodic output for the InnoDB Lock Monitor.

innodb_strict_mode.name: innodb_strict_mode

innodb_support_xa.name: innodb_support_xa
innodb_support_xa.description: Enable InnoDB support for the XA two-phase commit

innodb_sync_array_size.name: innodb_sync_array_size
innodb_sync_array_size.description: Splits an internal data structure used to coordinate threads, for higher concurrency in workloads with large numbers of waiting threads

innodb_sync_spin_loops.name: innodb_sync_spin_loops
innodb_sync_spin_loops.description: Count of spin-loop rounds in InnoDB mutexes

innodb_table_locks.name: innodb_table_locks
innodb_table_locks.description: Enable InnoDB locking in LOCK TABLES

innodb_temp_data_file_path.name: innodb_temp_data_file_path
innodb_temp_data_file_path.description: The path and size of the InnoDB temporary tablespace data file.

internal_tmp_mem_storage_engine.name: internal_tmp_mem_storage_engine
internal_tmp_mem_storage_engine.description: Defines the storage to use for internal in-memory temporary tables

innodb_tmpdir.name: innodb_tmpdir
innodb_tmpdir.description: The directory location for the temporary table files created during online ALTER TABLE operations

innodb_thread_concurrency.name: innodb_thread_concurrency
innodb_thread_concurrency.description: Sets the maximum number of threads allowed inside InnoDB. Value 0 will disable the thread throttling

innodb_thread_concurrency_timer_based.name: innodb_thread_concurrency_timer_based
innodb_thread_concurrency_timer_based.description: Whether to use the lock-free method of handling thread concurrency

innodb_thread_sleep_delay.name: innodb_thread_sleep_delay
innodb_thread_sleep_delay.description: Time, in microseconds, that an InnoDB thread sleeps before joining InnoDB queue. Value 0 disables the sleep behavior

innodb_trx_purge_view_update_only_debug.name: innodb_trx_purge_view_update_only_debug
innodb_trx_purge_view_update_only_debug.description: Pauses purging of delete-marked records while allowing the purge view to be updated

innodb_trx_rseg_n_slots_debug.name: innodb_trx_rseg_n_slots_debug
innodb_trx_rseg_n_slots_debug.description: Sets a debug flag that limits TRX_RSEG_N_SLOTS to a given value for the trx_rsegf_undo_find_free function

innodb_undo_directory.name: innodb_undo_directory
innodb_undo_directory.description: The relative or absolute directory path where InnoDB creates separate tablespaces for the undo logs; typically used to place those logs on a different storage device

innodb_undo_log_encrypt.name: innodb_undo_log_encrypt
innodb_undo_log_encrypt.description: Controls encryption of undo log data for encrypted tablepaces

innodb_undo_log_truncate.name: innodb_undo_log_truncate
innodb_undo_log_truncate.description: Enable this option to mark the InnoDB undo tablespace for truncation

innodb_undo_logs.name: innodb_undo_logs
innodb_undo_logs.description: Defines the number of undo logs (rollback segments) used by InnoDB; an alias for innodb_rollback_segments

innodb_undo_tablespaces.name: innodb_undo_tablespaces
innodb_undo_tablespaces.description: The number of tablespace files that rollback segments are divided between

innodb_use_legacy_cardinality_algorithm.name: innodb_use_legacy_cardinality_algorithm
innodb_use_legacy_cardinality_algorithm.description: Whether to use legacy InnoDB index cardinality calculation algorithm

innodb_use_native_aio.name: innodb_use_native_aio
innodb_use_native_aio.description: Specifies whether to use the Linux asynchronous I/O subsystem

innodb_use_sys_malloc.name: innodb_use_sys_malloc
innodb_use_sys_malloc.description: Whether InnoDB uses the OS or its own memory allocator

innodb_write_io_threads.name: innodb_write_io_threads
innodb_write_io_threads.description: The number of I/O threads for write operations in InnoDB. Range is 1-64.

insert_id.name: insert_id
insert_id.description: Set the value to be used by the following INSERT or ALTER TABLE statement when inserting an AUTO_INCREMENT value

interactive_timeout.name: interactive_timeout
interactive_timeout.description: The number of seconds the server waits for activity on an interactive connection before closing it. Applies to TCP/IP and Unix socket connections.

internal_tmp_disk_storage_engine.name: internal_tmp_disk_storage_engine
internal_tmp_disk_storage_engine.description: Storage engine for internal temporary tables

join_buffer_size.name: join_buffer_size
join_buffer_size.description: The minimum size of the buffer used for plain index scans and range index scans. Used for joins without indexes.

key_cache_age_threshold.name: key_cache_age_threshold

key_cache_block_size.name: key_cache_block_size

key_cache_division_limit.name: key_cache_division_limit
key_cache_division_limit.description: The minimum percentage of warm blocks in key cache

keyring_aws_cmk_id.name: keyring_aws_cmk_id
keyring_aws_cmk_id.description: AWS keyring plugin customer master key ID value

keyring_aws_conf_file.name: keyring_aws_conf_file
keyring_aws_conf_file.description: AWS keyring plugin configuration file location

keyring_aws_data_file.name: keyring_aws_data_file
keyring_aws_data_file.description: AWS keyring plugin storage file location

keyring_okv_conf_dir.name: keyring_okv_conf_dir
keyring_okv_conf_dir.description: Oracle Key Vault keyring plugin configuration directory

lc-messages-dir.name: lc-messages-dir

authentication_ldap_sasl_ca_path.description: LDAP server certificate authority file name

authentication_ldap_sasl_init_pool_size.description: LDAP server initial connection pool size

authentication_ldap_sasl_tls.description: Whether to use encrypted connections to LDAP server

authentication_ldap_simple_tls.description: Whether to use encrypted connections to LDAP server

log-bin-use-v1-row-events.name: log-bin-use-v1-row-events
log-bin-use-v1-row-events.description: Use version 1 binary log row events

log-output.name: log-output
log-output.description: The destination for general query log and slow query log output

log-queries-not-using-indexes.name: log-queries-not-using-indexes
log-queries-not-using-indexes.description: Log queries that are executed without benefit of any index to the slow query log if it is open

log-slave-updates.description: Tells the slave to log the updates performed by its SQL thread to its own binary log

maria-repair-threads.name: maria-repair-threads

max_binlog_stmt_cache_size.name: max_binlog_stmt_cache_size
max_binlog_stmt_cache_size.description: Can be used to restrict the total size used to cache all nontransactional statements during a transaction

max_length_for_sort_data.description: Max number of bytes in sorted records

max_long_data_size.description: Max size of parameter values that mysql_stmt_send_long_data() can send

max_points_in_geometry.name: max_points_in_geometry

mecab_rc_file.name: mecab_rc_file

metadata_locks_cache_size.name: metadata_locks_cache_size
metadata_locks_cache_size.description: Size of the metadata locks cache

min-examined-row-limit.name: min-examined-row-limit

multi_range_count.name: multi_range_count

myisam_data_pointer_size.name: myisam_data_pointer_size
myisam_data_pointer_size.description: Default pointer size to be used for MyISAM tables

myisam_max_sort_file_size.name: myisam_max_sort_file_size
myisam_max_sort_file_size.description: Do not use the fast sort index method to create index if the temporary file would get bigger than this

myisam_mmap_size.name: myisam_mmap_size
myisam_mmap_size.description: The maximum amount of memory to use for memory mapping compressed MyISAM files

ndb-log-update-as-write.name: ndb-log-update-as-write
ndb-log-update-as-write.description: Toggles logging of updates on the master between updates (OFF) and writes (ON)

ndb-wait-connected.name: ndb-wait-connected
ndb-wait-connected.description: Time (in seconds) for the MySQL server to wait for connection to cluster management and data nodes before accepting MySQL client connections

ndb-wait-setup.name: ndb-wait-setup
ndb-wait-setup.description: Time (in seconds) for the MySQL server to wait for NDB engine setup to complete

ndb-allow-copying-alter-table.name: ndb-allow-copying-alter-table
ndb-allow-copying-alter-table.description: Set to OFF to keep ALTER TABLE from using copying operations on NDB tables

ndb_cache_check_time.name: ndb_cache_check_time
ndb_cache_check_time.description: Number of milliseconds between checks of cluster SQL nodes made by the MySQL query cache

ndb_clear_apply_status.name: ndb_clear_apply_status
ndb_clear_apply_status.description: Causes RESET SLAVE to clear all rows from the ndb_apply_status table; ON by default

Ndb_conflict_last_conflict_epoch.name: Ndb_conflict_last_conflict_epoch
Ndb_conflict_last_conflict_epoch.description: Most recent NDB epoch on this slave in which a conflict was detected

ndb_data_node_neighbour.name: ndb_data_node_neighbour
ndb_data_node_neighbour.description: Specifies cluster data node "closest" to this MySQL Server, for transaction hinting and fully replicated tables

ndb_default_column_format.name: ndb_default_column_format
ndb_default_column_format.description: Sets default row format and column format (FIXED or DYNAMIC) used for new NDB tables

ndb_deferred_constraints.name: ndb_deferred_constraints
ndb_deferred_constraints.description: Specifies that constraint checks should be deferred (where these are supported). Not normally needed or used; for testing purposes only.

ndb_distribution.name: ndb_distribution
ndb_distribution.description: Default distribution for new tables in NDBCLUSTER (KEYHASH or LINHASH, default is KEYHASH)

ndb_eventbuffer_free_percent.name: ndb_eventbuffer_free_percent
ndb_eventbuffer_free_percent.description: Percentage of free memory that should be available in event buffer before resumption of buffering, after reaching limit set by ndb_eventbuffer_max_alloc

ndb_eventbuffer_max_alloc.name: ndb_eventbuffer_max_alloc
ndb_eventbuffer_max_alloc.description: Maximum memory that can be allocated for buffering events by the NDB API. Defaults to 0 (no limit).

ndb_extra_logging.name: ndb_extra_logging
ndb_extra_logging.description: Controls logging of NDB Cluster schema, connection, and data distribution events in the MySQL error log

ndb_force_send.name: ndb_force_send
ndb_force_send.description: Forces sending of buffers to NDB immediately, without waiting for other threads

ndb_fully_replicated.name: ndb_fully_replicated
ndb_fully_replicated.description: Whether new NDB tables are fully replicated

ndb_index_stat_cache_entries.name: ndb_index_stat_cache_entries
ndb_index_stat_cache_entries.description: Sets the granularity of the statistics by determining the number of starting and ending keys

ndb_index_stat_enable.name: ndb_index_stat_enable
ndb_index_stat_enable.description: Use NDB index statistics in query optimization

ndb_index_stat_option.name: ndb_index_stat_option
ndb_index_stat_option.description: Comma-separated list of tunable options for NDB index statistics; the list should contain no spaces

ndb_index_stat_update_freq.name: ndb_index_stat_update_freq
ndb_index_stat_update_freq.description: How often to query data nodes instead of the statistics cache

ndb_join_pushdown.name: ndb_join_pushdown
ndb_join_pushdown.description: Enables pushing down of joins to data nodes

ndb_log_apply_status.name: ndb_log_apply_status
ndb_log_apply_status.description: Whether or not a MySQL server acting as a slave logs mysql.ndb_apply_status updates received from its immediate master in its own binary log, using its own server ID

ndb_log_bin.name: ndb_log_bin
ndb_log_bin.description: Write updates to NDB tables in the binary log. Effective only if binary logging is enabled with --log-bin.

ndb_log_binlog_index.name: ndb_log_binlog_index
ndb_log_binlog_index.description: Insert mapping between epochs and binary log positions into the ndb_binlog_index table. Defaults to ON. Effective only if binary logging is enabled on the server.

ndb_log_empty_epochs.name: ndb_log_empty_epochs
ndb_log_empty_epochs.description: When enabled, epochs in which there were no changes are written to the ndb_apply_status and ndb_binlog_index tables, even when log_slave_updates is enabled

ndb_log_empty_update.name: ndb_log_empty_update
ndb_log_empty_update.description: When enabled, updates which produce no changes are written to the ndb_apply_status and ndb_binlog_index tables, even when log_slave_updates is enabled

ndb_log_exclusive_reads.name: ndb_log_exclusive_reads
ndb_log_exclusive_reads.description: Log primary key reads with exclusive locks; allow conflict resolution based on read conflicts

ndb_log_orig.name: ndb_log_orig
ndb_log_orig.description: Whether the id and epoch of the originating server are recorded in the mysql.ndb_binlog_index table. Set using the --ndb-log-orig option when starting mysqld.

ndb_log_transaction_id.name: ndb_log_transaction_id
ndb_log_transaction_id.description: Whether NDB transaction IDs are written into the binary log (Read-only.)

ndb-log-update-minimal.name: ndb-log-update-minimal
ndb-log-update-minimal.description: Log updates in a minimal format.

ndb_log_updated_only.name: ndb_log_updated_only
ndb_log_updated_only.description: Log complete rows (ON) or updates only (OFF)

ndb_optimization_delay.name: ndb_optimization_delay
ndb_optimization_delay.description: Sets the number of milliseconds to wait between processing sets of rows by OPTIMIZE TABLE on NDB tables

ndb_optimized_node_selection.name: ndb_optimized_node_selection
ndb_optimized_node_selection.description: Determines how an SQL node chooses a cluster data node to use as transaction coordinator

ndb_recv_thread_cpu_mask.name: ndb_recv_thread_cpu_mask
ndb_recv_thread_cpu_mask.description: CPU mask for locking receiver threads to specific CPUs; specified as hexadecimal. See documentation for details.

ndb_report_thresh_binlog_epoch_slip.name: ndb_report_thresh_binlog_epoch_slip
ndb_report_thresh_binlog_epoch_slip.description: NDB 7.5.4 and later: Threshold for number of epochs completely buffered, but not yet consumed by binlog injector thread which when exceeded generates BUFFERED_EPOCHS_OVER_THRESHOLD event buffer status message; prior to NDB 7.5.4: Threshold for number of epochs to lag behind before reporting binary log status

ndb_report_thresh_binlog_mem_usage.name: ndb_report_thresh_binlog_mem_usage
ndb_report_thresh_binlog_mem_usage.description: This is a threshold on the percentage of free memory remaining before reporting binary log status

ndb_show_foreign_key_mock_tables.name: ndb_show_foreign_key_mock_tables
ndb_show_foreign_key_mock_tables.description: Show the mock tables used to support foreign_key_checks=0

ndb_slave_conflict_role.name: ndb_slave_conflict_role

Ndb_slave_max_replicated_epoch.name: Ndb_slave_max_replicated_epoch
Ndb_slave_max_replicated_epoch.description: The most recently committed NDB epoch on this slave. When this value is greater than or equal to Ndb_conflict_last_conflict_epoch, no conflicts have yet been detected.

Ndb_system_name.name: Ndb_system_name
Ndb_system_name.description: Configured cluster system name; empty if server not connected to NDB

ndb_table_no_logging.name: ndb_table_no_logging
ndb_table_no_logging.description: NDB tables created when this setting is enabled are not checkpointed to disk (although table schema files are created). The setting in effect when the table is created with or altered to use NDBCLUSTER persists for the lifetime of the table.

ndb_table_temporary.name: ndb_table_temporary
ndb_table_temporary.description: NDB tables are not persistent on disk: no schema files are created and the tables are not logged

ndb_use_copying_alter_table.name: ndb_use_copying_alter_table
ndb_use_copying_alter_table.description: Use copying ALTER TABLE operations in NDB Cluster

ndb_use_exact_count.name: ndb_use_exact_count

ndb_use_transactions.name: ndb_use_transactions

ndb_version.name: ndb_version

net_write_timeout.name: net_write_timeout

ngram_token_size.name: ngram_token_size

old.name: old

old_passwords.name: old_passwords
old_passwords.description: Selects password hashing method for PASSWORD()

open-files-limit.name: open-files-limit

server-id-bits.description: Sets the number of least significant bits in the server_id actually used for identifying the server, permitting NDB API applications to store application data in the most significant bits. server_id must be less than 2 to the power of this value.

server_id_bits.name: server_id_bits
server_id_bits.description: The effective value of server_id if the server was started with the --server-id-bits option set to a nondefault value

session_track_gtids.name: session_track_gtids

session_track_state_change.description: Whether to track session state changes

session_track_system_variables.description: Session variables to track changes for

sha256_password_auto_generate_rsa_keys.description: Whether to autogenerate RSA key-pair files

sha256_password_private_key_path.description: SHA256 authentication plugin private key path name

sha256_password_proxy_users.name: sha256_password_proxy_users
sha256_password_proxy_users.description: Whether the sha256_password authentication plugin does proxying

sha256_password_public_key_path.name: sha256_password_public_key_path
sha256_password_public_key_path.description: SHA256 authentication plugin public key path name

shared_memory.name: shared_memory

shared_memory_base_name.name: shared_memory_base_name
shared_memory_base_name.description: The name of shared memory to use for shared-memory connections

show_create_table_verbosity.name: show_create_table_verbosity
show_create_table_verbosity.description: Whether to display ROW_FORMAT in SHOW CREATE TABLE even if it has the default value

show_old_temporals.name: show_old_temporals
show_old_temporals.description: Whether SHOW CREATE TABLE should indicate pre-5.6.4 temporal columns

simplified_binlog_gtid_recovery.name: simplified_binlog_gtid_recovery
simplified_binlog_gtid_recovery.description: Controls how binary logs are iterated during GTID recovery

skip-networking.name: skip-networking
skip-networking.description: Do not allow connection with TCP/IP

skip-show-database.name: skip-show-database
skip-show-database.description: Do not allow SHOW DATABASE statements

slave-load-tmpdir.name: slave-load-tmpdir
slave-load-tmpdir.description: The location where the slave should put its temporary files when replicating a LOAD DATA INFILE statement

slave-net-timeout.name: slave-net-timeout
slave-net-timeout.description: Number of seconds to wait for more data from a master/slave connection before aborting the read

slave-skip-errors.name: slave-skip-errors
slave-skip-errors.description: Tells the slave thread to continue replication when a query returns an error from the provided list

slave_allow_batching.name: slave_allow_batching
slave_allow_batching.description: Turns update batching on and off for a replication slave

slave_checkpoint_group.name: slave_checkpoint_group
slave_checkpoint_group.description: Maximum number of transactions processed by a multi-threaded slave before a checkpoint operation is called to update progress status. Not supported by NDB Cluster.

slave_checkpoint_period.name: slave_checkpoint_period
slave_checkpoint_period.description: Update progress status of multi-threaded slave and flush relay log info to disk after this number of milliseconds. Not supported by NDB Cluster.

slave_exec_mode.name: slave_exec_mode
slave_exec_mode.description: Allows for switching the slave thread between IDEMPOTENT mode (key and some other errors suppressed) and STRICT mode; STRICT mode is the default, except for NDB Cluster, where IDEMPOTENT is always used

slave_max_allowed_packet.name: slave_max_allowed_packet
slave_max_allowed_packet.description: Maximum size, in bytes, of a packet that can be sent from a replication master to a slave; overrides max_allowed_packet

slave_parallel_type.name: slave_parallel_type
slave_parallel_type.description: Tells the slave to use timestamp information (LOGICAL_CLOCK) or database partioning (DATABASE) to parallelize transactions.

slave_parallel_workers.name: slave_parallel_workers
slave_parallel_workers.description: Number of applier threads for executing replication transactions in parallel. A value of 0 disables slave multi-threading. Not supported by MySQL Cluster.

slave_pending_jobs_size_max.name: slave_pending_jobs_size_max
slave_pending_jobs_size_max.description: Maximum size of slave worker queues holding events not yet applied

slave_preserve_commit_order.name: slave_preserve_commit_order
slave_preserve_commit_order.description: Ensures that all commits by slave workers happen in the same order as on the master to maintain consistency when using parallel applier threads.

slave_rows_search_algorithms.name: slave_rows_search_algorithms
slave_rows_search_algorithms.description: Determines search algorithms used for slave update batching. Any 2 or 3 from the list INDEX_SEARCH, TABLE_SCAN, HASH_SCAN.

slave_sql_verify_checksum.name: slave_sql_verify_checksum
slave_sql_verify_checksum.description: Cause slave to examine checksums when reading from relay log

slave_transaction_retries.name: slave_transaction_retries
slave_transaction_retries.description: Number of times the slave SQL thread will retry a transaction in case it failed with a deadlock or elapsed lock wait timeout, before giving up and stopping

slave_type_conversions.name: slave_type_conversions
slave_type_conversions.description: Controls type conversion mode on replication slave. Value is a list of zero or more elements from the list: ALL_LOSSY, ALL_NON_LOSSY. Set to an empty string to disallow type conversions between master and slave.

slow_launch_time.name: slow_launch_time
slow_launch_time.description: If creating the thread takes longer than this value (in seconds), the Slow_launch_threads counter will be incremented

slow_query_log_file.name: slow_query_log_file
slow_query_log_file.description: The name of the slow query log file. The path is relative to the data directory unless an absolute path is given.

socket.name: socket
socket.description: Socket file on which to listen for Unix socket or Windows named pipe connections

sort_buffer_size.name: sort_buffer_size
sort_buffer_size.description: Each session that must perform a sort allocates a buffer of this size. Setting large values can improve large sorts.

sql_auto_is_null.name: sql_auto_is_null
sql_auto_is_null.description: If set to 1, you can find the last inserted row for a table that contains an AUTO_INCREMENT column by using the following construct: WHERE auto_increment_column IS NULL

sql_big_selects.name: sql_big_selects
sql_big_selects.description: If set to 0, MySQL aborts SELECT statements that are likely to take a very long time to execute

sql_big_tables.name: sql_big_tables
sql_big_tables.description: This variable is deprecated, and is mapped to big_tables

sql_buffer_result.name: sql_buffer_result
sql_buffer_result.description: Forces the result to be put into a temporary table

sql_log_off.name: sql_log_off
sql_log_off.description: Whether logging to the general query log is disabled for the current connection. Requires the SUPER privilege.

sql_log_update.name: sql_log_update
sql_log_update.description: This variable is deprecated, and is mapped to SQL_LOG_BIN

sql_low_priority_updates.name: sql_low_priority_updates
sql_low_priority_updates.description: This variable is deprecated, and is mapped to low_priority_updates

sql_max_join_size.name: sql_max_join_size
sql_max_join_size.description: This variable is deprecated, and is mapped to max_join_size

sql_notes.name: sql_notes
sql_notes.description: If set to 1, warnings of Note level are recorded

sql_quote_show_create.name: sql_quote_show_create
sql_quote_show_create.description: If set to 1 the server quotes identifiers for SHOW CREATE TABLE and SHOW CREATE DATABASE statements

sql_safe_updates.name: sql_safe_updates
sql_safe_updates.description: If set to 1, MySQL aborts UPDATE or DELETE statements that do not use a key in the WHERE clause or a LIMIT clause

sql_select_limit.name: sql_select_limit
sql_select_limit.description: The maximum number of rows to return from SELECT statements

sql_slave_skip_counter.name: sql_slave_skip_counter
sql_slave_skip_counter.description: Number of events from the master that a slave server should skip. Not compatible with GTID replication.

sql_warnings.name: sql_warnings
sql_warnings.description: This variable controls whether single-row INSERT statements produce an information string if warnings occur

ssl-ca.name: ssl-ca

ssl-capath.name: ssl-capath
ssl-capath.description: Directory that contains trusted SSL Certificate Authority certificate files

ssl-cipher.name: ssl-cipher
ssl-cipher.description: List of permitted ciphers for connection encryption

ssl-crl.name: ssl-crl

ssl-crlpath.description: Directory that contains certificate revocation list files

ssl_fips_mode.name: ssl_fips_mode
ssl_fips_mode.description: Whether to enable FIPS mode on the server side

stored_program_cache.name: stored_program_cache

stored_program_definition_cache.name: stored_program_definition_cache
stored_program_definition_cache.description: Defines the number of stored program and event definition objects that can be kept in the dictionary object cache

super_read_only.name: super_read_only
super_read_only.description: When enabled, the server denies all updates, including from users with SUPER privilege. Used for read-only replicas.

sync-bdb-logs.name: sync-bdb-logs
sync-bdb-logs.description: Synchronously flush Berkeley DB logs. Enabled by default

sync_binlog.name: sync_binlog
sync_binlog.description: Controls the number of binary log commits that are synchronized to disk. Setting to 1 provides the highest durability.

sync_frm.name: sync_frm
sync_frm.description: Sync .frm to disk on create. Enabled by default

sync_master_info.name: sync_master_info
sync_master_info.description: Synchronize master.info to disk after every #th event

sync_relay_log.name: sync_relay_log
sync_relay_log.description: Synchronize relay log to disk after every #th event

sync_relay_log_info.name: sync_relay_log_info
sync_relay_log_info.description: Synchronize relay.info file to disk after every #th event

system_time_zone.name: system_time_zone

Ssl_session_cache_timeouts.name: Ssl_session_cache_timeouts
Ssl_session_cache_timeouts.description: Number of SSL session cache timeouts

Ssl_sessions_reused.name: Ssl_sessions_reused
Ssl_sessions_reused.description: How many SSL connections were reused from the cache

Ssl_used_session_cache_entries.name: Ssl_used_session_cache_entries
Ssl_used_session_cache_entries.description: How many SSL session cache entries were used

Ssl_verify_depth.name: Ssl_verify_depth
Ssl_verify_depth.description: The verification depth for replication SSL connections

Ssl_verify_mode.name: Ssl_verify_mode
Ssl_verify_mode.description: The verification mode for replication SSL connections

Table_locks_immediate.name: Table_locks_immediate
Table_locks_immediate.description: Number of times that a table lock was acquired immediately

Table_locks_waited.name: Table_locks_waited
Table_locks_waited.description: Number of times that a table lock could not be acquired immediately and a wait was needed

Table_open_cache_hits.name: Table_open_cache_hits
Table_open_cache_hits.description: Number of hits for open tables cache lookups

Table_open_cache_misses.name: Table_open_cache_misses
Table_open_cache_misses.description: Number of misses for open tables cache lookups

Table_open_cache_overflows.name: Table_open_cache_overflows
Table_open_cache_overflows.description: Number of overflows for the open tables cache

Tc_log_max_pages_used.name: Tc_log_max_pages_used
Tc_log_max_pages_used.description: When the memory-mapped implementation of the log that is used by mysqld acts as the transaction coordinator for recovery of internal XA transactions,this variable indicates the largest number of pages used for the log since the server started

Tc_log_page_size.name: Tc_log_page_size
Tc_log_page_size.description: The page size used for the memory-mapped implementation of the XA recovery log

Tc_log_page_waits.name: Tc_log_page_waits
Tc_log_page_waits.description: For the memory-mapped implementation of the recovery log, this variable increments each time the server was not able to commit a transaction and had to wait for a free page in the log

Threads_cached.name: Threads_cached
Threads_cached.description: Number of threads in the thread cache

Threads_created.name: Threads_created
Threads_created.description: Number of threads created to handle connections

Threads_running.name: Threads_running
Threads_running.description: Number of threads that are not sleeping

Uptime.name: Uptime
Uptime.description: Number of seconds the server has been up

Uptime_since_flush_status.name: Uptime_since_flush_status
Uptime_since_flush_status.description: Number of seconds since the most recent FLUSH STATUS

validate_password.dictionary_file_last_p.name: validate_password.dictionary_file_last_parsed
validate_password.dictionary_file_last_p.description: When the dictionary file was last parsed

validate_password.dictionary_file_words_.name: validate_password.dictionary_file_words_count
validate_password.dictionary_file_words_.description: Number of words in dictionary file

validate_password_dictionary_file_last_p.name: validate_password_dictionary_file_last_parsed
validate_password_dictionary_file_last_p.description: When the dictionary file was last parsed

validate_password_dictionary_file_words_.name: validate_password_dictionary_file_words_count
validate_password_dictionary_file_words_.description: Number of words in dictionary file

connect_timeout.name: connect_timeout
connect_timeout.description: The number of seconds the mysqld server waits for a connect packet before responding with a Bad handshake. Used during the initial connection handshake.

slow_query_log.name: slow_query_log
slow_query_log.description: Whether the slow query log is enabled. When enabled, queries that take longer than long_query_time seconds are logged.

log_queries_not_using_indexes.name: log_queries_not_using_indexes
log_queries_not_using_indexes.description: Whether queries that do not use indexes are logged to the slow query log. Requires slow_query_log to be enabled.

general_log.name: general_log
general_log.description: Whether the general query log is enabled. Logs all SQL queries received from clients. Can generate large log files.

ssl_ca.name: ssl_ca
ssl_ca.description: The path to a file in PEM format that contains a list of trusted SSL certificate authorities.

ssl_capath.name: ssl_capath
ssl_capath.description: The path to a directory that contains trusted SSL certificate authority certificates in PEM format.

ssl_cert.name: ssl_cert
ssl_cert.description: The name of the SSL certificate file in PEM format to use for establishing encrypted connections.

ssl_key.name: ssl_key
ssl_key.description: The name of the SSL key file in PEM format to use for establishing encrypted connections.

ssl_cipher.name: ssl_cipher
ssl_cipher.description: A list of permitted ciphers or cipher suites for encrypted connections.

ssl_crl.name: ssl_crl
ssl_crl.description: The path to a file containing certificate revocation lists for SSL connections.

ssl_crlpath.name: ssl_crlpath
ssl_crlpath.description: The path to a directory containing SSL certificate revocation list files.

server_id.name: server_id
server_id.description: The server ID uniquely identifies the MySQL server instance. Must be unique among servers in a replication topology.

binlog_format.name: binlog_format
binlog_format.description: The format of binary log entries: ROW, STATEMENT, or MIXED. Determines how SQL statements are logged for replication.

relay_log.name: relay_log
relay_log.description: The name of the relay log file for replication. Includes base name and optional path.

character_set_server.name: character_set_server
character_set_server.description: The default character set for the server. Used when no character set is specified in CREATE DATABASE or CREATE TABLE.

collation_server.name: collation_server
collation_server.description: The default collation for the server. Used when no collation is specified in CREATE DATABASE or CREATE TABLE.

transaction_isolation.name: transaction_isolation
transaction_isolation.description: The default transaction isolation level. Values: READ-UNCOMMITTED, READ-COMMITTED, REPEATABLE-READ, SERIALIZABLE.

sql_mode.name: sql_mode
sql_mode.description: The current SQL mode. Controls server SQL behaviors such as strict mode, zero dates, and automatic type casting.

default_storage_engine.name: default_storage_engine
default_storage_engine.description: The default storage engine for new tables. Set to InnoDB for MySQL 5.5+ by default.
